why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound keep_alive must dispatch from the official framed packet id to the keep-alive body decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is the packet-level prerequisite for a later runtime keep-alive echo harness.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_keepalive_clientbound_framed_dispatch_decodes_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 4, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x04.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode, CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets, and ClientboundKeepAlivePacket.getId().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration mapping contains clientbound finish_configuration id 0x03 but not keep_alive id 0x04.
